data.Substance_Name: 1,2-Benzenedicarboxylic acid, polymer with (2Z)-2-butenedioic acid and ethenylbenzene
data.Registry_Name: 1,2-Benzenedicarboxylic acid, polymer with (2Z)-2-butenedioic acid and ethenylbenzene

data.Definition: A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced by distillation of crude oil.  It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C6 through C12 and boiling in the range of approximately 65.degree.C to 230.degree.C (149.degree.F to 446.degree.F).

A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced as the lowest boiling stream in the vacuum distillation of the residuum from atmospheric distillation of crude oil.  It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C11 through C25 and boiling in the range of approximately 205.degree.C to 400.degree.C (401.degree.F to 752.degree.F).

A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced by vacuum distillation of the residuum from atmospheric distillation of crude oil.  It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C15 through C30 and produces a finished oil with a viscosity of less than 100 SUS at 100.degree.F (19cSt at 40.degree.C).  It contains a relatively large proportion of saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons normally present in this distillation range of crude oil.

A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced by vacuum distillation of the residuum from atmospheric distillation of crude oil.  It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C20 through C50 and produces a finished oil with a viscosity of at least 100 SUS at 100.degree.F (19cSt at 40.degree.C).  It contains a relatively large proportion of saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons.

A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced by distillation of crude oil.  It consists of aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C4 through C10 and boiling in the range of approximately -20 .degree.C to 180.degree.C (-4.degree.F to 356.degree.F).

A complex combination of hydrocarbons separated as a liquid from natural gas in a surface separator by retrograde condensation.  It consists mainly of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C2 to C20.  It is a liquid at atmospheric temperature and pressure.

A complex combination of hydrocarbons separated as a liquid from natural gas in a gas recycling plant by processes such as refrigeration or absorption.  It consists mainly of saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C2 through C8.

A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced by the distillation of crude oil.  It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C11 through C20 and boiling in the range of 205.degree.C to 345.degree.C (401.degree.F to 653.degree.F).

A complex residuum from the atmospheric distillation of crude oil. It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly greater than C20 and boiling above approximately 350.degree.C (662.degree.F).  This stream is likely to contain 5 wt. % or more of 4- to 6-membered condensed ring aromatic hydrocarbons.

A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced by distillation of crude oil.  It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C4 through C11 and boiling in the range of approximately minus 20.degree.C to 220.degree.C (-4.degree.F to 428.degree.F).

A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced by the distillation of crude oil.  It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C11 through C25 and boiling in the range of approximately 205.degree.C to 400.degree.C (401.degree.F to 752.degree.F).

A complex combination of hydrocarbons produced by the distillation of products from a catalytic cracking process.  It consists of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C9 through C25 and boiling in the range of approximately 150.degree.C to 400.degree.C (302.degree.F to 752.degree.F).  It contains a relatively large proportion of bicyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.
